Resolves #15144

Promotes the v13.2 dense-workspace flagship from the unrelated AgentOS dock-demo `?demo=c` mode into a complete standalone Workstation application at `/apps/workstation/index.html`. The extraction relocates the existing workspace, panes, `Store<Model>` state, tour, SCSS, and tests under `Workstation.*`; gives both Neo themes app-owned `--workstation-*` tokens; generalizes the story away from Fleet/AgentOS product claims; and leaves Demo A/B as the only dockdemo modes.

The proof itself is preserved rather than forked: twenty panes, exactly 100,000 scale records, the capped ten-records/sec feed, one root `state.Provider`, real overflow, split/return choreography, dual themes, Canvas-worker output, and component/store/DOM identity continuity all run from the standalone boot surface. The generic `neo.harness.dockZone.v1` schema remains unchanged and no wrapper, redirect, re-export, or duplicate implementation survives at the former AgentOS path.

Evidence: L3 (mounted Chromium plus Neural Link inspection and sequential-frame sampling of the standalone App Worker, stores, DOM, Canvas output, themes, docking, overflow, and #15143 clip-safe staging) ŌåÆ L3 required (the flagship's acceptance boundary is browser-reachable runtime behavior). No residuals.

## Source of Authority

- The operator's 2026-07-14 product-boundary correction and issue #15144 supersede closed #15099's placement premise while preserving its accepted feature proof.
- `apps/colors` / `apps/finance` remain the sibling authority for top-level app placement; core dashboard, state, grid, DockService, DockZoneModel, and TourRunner remain the mechanism authorities.
- The branch is rebased onto landed PR #15143; the relocated Workstation journey carries its stronger fixed-stage paint, clipping, active-header, overflow, and cleanup assertions.

## Deltas from ticket

- No intentional scope delta. The durable application name is `Workstation`; ŌĆ£denseŌĆØ remains an acceptance property, and `neo.harness.dockZone.v1` remains the generic core schema as prescribed.
- The theme map is regenerated from a clean source scan during validation so the deleted Demo C entry cannot survive as stale generated metadata; generated development artifacts remain untracked.

## Test Evidence

- Theme discovery: `npm run build-themes -- -f -n -e dev` ŌĆö passed; the generated map contains `apps.workstation.Viewport/Workspace`, preserves Demo A/B, and contains no Demo C entry.
- Focused unit preservation: `npm run test-unit -- ...DemoAWorkspace ...DemoBWorkspace ...demoADockChoreography ...workstation/Workspace ...workstation/tour/denseWorkstation` ŌĆö 34 passed.
- Standalone unit retest after the final ownership census: focused Workstation workspace/tour command ŌĆö 4 passed.
- Standalone mounted journey after rebasing onto #15143: `NEO_E2E_PORT=8124 npx playwright test workstation/WorkstationNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1` ŌĆö 1 passed in 14.9s; both fixed-stage bursts remained painted and identity-stable with hidden overflow and zero residue.
- AgentOS preservation: `NEO_E2E_PORT=8125 npx playwright test dashboard/DemoATourNL agentos/DemoBPerspectivesNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1` ŌĆö 2 passed; Demo A choreography and Demo B perspective/pop-out behavior remain live.
- Ownership census: case-insensitive Workstation app/theme/test scan found no AgentOS, Demo C, Fleet, `--fm-*`, Memory Core, or Native Edge Graph identity; only the ticket-approved generic schema string remains.
- Repository gates: repair-capable and check-only `npm run agent-preflight` passed; commit hooks passed whitespace, shorthand, JSDoc types, ticket archaeology, block alignment, parsing, and test-mutation checks.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Open `/apps/workstation/index.html` on merged `dev`, run the full tour, and confirm both themes plus the two clip-safe fixed-stage bursts remain residue-free.
- [ ] Open the AgentOS dockdemo default and `?demo=b` routes and confirm no Demo C mode or import remains.
